## Figure Type
Geophysical image map — specifically a radiometric or aeromagnetic (most likely **radiometric ternary or total magnetic intensity**) raster image overlaid with tenement/mining lease boundaries, prospect locations, and geographic annotations. The colour gradient background represents a continuous geophysical dataset rendered in false colour.

---

## Coverage and Subject
The map covers an area in the **Murchison region of Western Australia**, centred approximately on the **Emily Well** area. The coordinate system visible is **MGA/GDA** with Easting ~585,000E and Northing ~6,975,000N (GDA94 Zone 50 or 51). The map spans roughly **15–20 km north–south and 20–25 km east–west** based on the scale bar showing 0–750–1,500 m (noting the bar appears relatively small relative to the coverage, suggesting a broader regional extent).

**Tenement labels visible:**
- Mining Leases: **M 20001242**, **M 20001285** (or similar)
- Exploration Licences/Prospecting Licences: **P 2002248, P 2002249, P 2002250, P 2002352, P 2002353, P 2002354, P 2002355, P 2002193, P 2002007**
- Exploration Licence: **E 2000871**

---

## Key Content

**Geophysical Background:**
The false-colour raster image displays a strongly **NNW–SSE trending linear fabric**, consistent with the structural grain of the **Murchison Domain** of the Yilgarn Craton. The colour pattern ranges from:
- **Deep blue/purple** — interpreted as low values (low magnetic response or low radioactivity), likely corresponding to mafic/ultramafic rocks or sediment cover
- **Green and yellow** — intermediate values
- **Red and orange** — high values, possibly felsic intrusives, granites, or potassic alteration zones
- **Bright red linear zones** — along the left (western) and right (eastern) margins, suggesting high-response linear structures, possibly shear zones or intrusive contacts

The dominant structural trend is clearly **NNW–SSE**, with sub-parallel linear anomalies that likely represent greenstone belt lithological contacts or regional shear zones. Two prominent **dashed white lines** trend NNW–SSE and likely represent interpreted **major faults or shear zones** (possibly the margins of a greenstone belt).

**Prospect/Deposit Markers:**
Yellow diamond symbols mark historical prospect locations and the red diamond marks the **Oxonian Pit** (the primary focus). Prospects are concentrated along the western dashed structural corridor:
- Western cluster: **Oxonian Pit** (red, labelled), Dryblowing, Gypsum, Oxonian Emily Well W, Asiatic, Kallarra, Emily Well, Emily Well SW, Premier, Treasure Trove, Treasure Trove North, Margaret, Paddys Reward, Coe, Malohno
- Central/eastern cluster: Jubilee, Alice Mary, White Hope, Antipodean, Hidden Treasure Extended, Hidden Treasure North Extended, Wallabie, Lady Nitouche, Hammerdown, Leviathan, Maralinga Downs, Saltire, Retreat, Warribrook, Emily Well E

Most gold prospects appear to align with the **NNW-trending structural corridors** visible in the geophysical data.

## Research Value

This figure provides a **regional geophysical context map** that spatially relates the **Oxonian Pit** and surrounding historical gold prospects to the underlying geophysical fabric of the terrane, revealing that mineralisation is strongly structurally controlled along **NNW-trending corridors** interpreted as shear zones within the Murchison greenstone belt. A geologist or AI research assistant would use this to identify **which tenements cover the most prospective geophysical signatures** (high-contrast contacts, shear zone intersections) and to prioritise target generation within the licence package. It directly addresses the question of **structural control on gold mineralisation** and helps define the regional framework for further exploration targeting within the Emily Well project area.
